title: pedicularis palustris subsp. palustris
intro: Pedicularis palustris subsp. palustris, also known as Marsh Lousewort, is a species of flowering plant in the bumblebee orchid family (Orobanchaceae). It is a perennial plant that obtains its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of surrounding plants, and has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been linked to several health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Marsh Lousewort can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
